req.title: System-Admin views leave-request
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a leave-request through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-016 requires a system-admin to view a leave-request via the UI with mandatory field validation and audit record creation. I designed five tests covering: (1) EP positive path – authenticated admin views a valid leave-request and gets HTTP 200/201 plus an audit entry; (2) NEG unauthenticated access returns 401; (3) NEG forbidden role (non-admin) returns 403; (4) BVA boundary – leave-request ID at the minimum valid boundary value (1) is viewable; (5) DT decision-table – missing mandatory field triggers 400 validation error. Together these cover all stated error pathways and the single happy path.

tests.name: test_admin_views_valid_leave_request
preconditions: At least one leave-request exists in the system
actions: Obtain a valid admin session token via <fixture:admin_auth_token>
Retrieve an existing leave-request ID via <helper_fn:get_existing_leave_request_id>
Navigate to /leave-requests/{id} using the Playwright page fixture
Assert HTTP response status is 200
Assert the leave-request entity ID is present on the page
Assert an audit record was written via <helper_fn:get_latest_audit_record>
expected: Audit record written with actor=system-admin and action=VIEW
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_views_valid_leave_request(admin_page, admin_auth_token, get_existing_leave_request_id, get_latest_audit_record, base_url):
    leave_id = get_existing_leave_request_id(admin_auth_token)
    assert leave_id is not None, "Precondition: leave-request must exist"

    response = admin_page.goto(f"{base_url}/leave-requests/{leave_id}")
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected 200, got {response.status}"

    expect(admin_page.locator("[data-testid='leave-request-id']")).to_be_visible()
    expect(admin_page.locator("[data-testid='leave-request-id']")).to_have_text(str(leave_id))

    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_id=leave_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written on successful view"
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "admin"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_leave_request
preconditions: No valid session token is present (unauthenticated user)
actions: Use a fresh Playwright browser context with no cookies or auth headers
Attempt to navigate to /leave-requests/{id}
Assert HTTP response status is 401 or page redirects to login
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_leave_request(known_leave_request_id, base_url, get_audit_record_count):
    before_count = get_audit_record_count(entity_id=known_leave_request_id, action="VIEW")

    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch()
        context = browser.new_context()  # no auth cookies
        page = context.new_page()
        response = page.goto(f"{base_url}/leave-requests/{known_leave_request_id}")

        is_401 = (response.status == 401)
        is_redirected = (page.url.rstrip("/").endswith("/login"))
        assert is_401 or is_redirected, (
            f"Expected 401 or /login redirect, got status={response.status} url={page.url}"
        )
        browser.close()

    after_count = get_audit_record_count(entity_id=known_leave_request_id, action="VIEW")
    assert after_count == before_count, "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated access"

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_viewing_leave_request
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role: employee (not admin)
actions: Obtain a valid employee session token via <fixture:employee_auth_token>
Use an employee-scoped Playwright page via <fixture:employee_page>
Navigate to /leave-requests/{id}
Assert HTTP response status is 403 or forbidden message is displayed
expected: HTTP 403 returned or forbidden UI message shown
No audit record written for VIEW action
trace: R-WEB-016 error pathway: 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_viewing_leave_request(employee_page, known_leave_request_id, base_url, get_audit_record_count):
    before_count = get_audit_record_count(entity_id=known_leave_request_id, action="VIEW")

    response = employee_page.goto(f"{base_url}/leave-requests/{known_leave_request_id}")

    is_403 = (response.status == 403)
    has_forbidden_text = employee_page.locator("text=/forbidden|not authorized|403/i").count() > 0
    assert is_403 or has_forbidden_text, (
        f"Expected 403 or forbidden message, got status={response.status}"
    )

    leave_details_visible = employee_page.locator("[data-testid='leave-request-id']").count() > 0
    assert not leave_details_visible, "Leave-request details must not be rendered for forbidden role"

    after_count = get_audit_record_count(entity_id=known_leave_request_id, action="VIEW")
    assert after_count == before_count, "No audit record should be written for forbidden access"

tests.name: test_admin_views_leave_request_with_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: A leave-request with the minimum valid ID (1) exists or is seeded
actions: Obtain a valid admin session token via <fixture:admin_auth_token>
Seed or confirm leave-request with ID=1 exists via <helper_fn:seed_leave_request_with_id>
Navigate to /leave-requests/1
Assert HTTP response status is 200
Assert leave-request entity with ID=1 is displayed
Assert audit record is written for entity_id=1
expected: HTTP 200 returned for boundary ID value of 1
Leave-request details for ID=1 rendered correctly
Audit record written with entity_id=1 and action=VIEW
trace: R-WEB-016 boundary: minimum valid leave-request ID
BVA lower boundary of entity identifier domain
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

BOUNDARY_ID = 1

def test_admin_views_leave_request_with_minimum_valid_id(admin_page, admin_auth_token, seed_leave_request_with_id, get_latest_audit_record, base_url):
    seed_leave_request_with_id(admin_auth_token, BOUNDARY_ID)

    response = admin_page.goto(f"{base_url}/leave-requests/{BOUNDARY_ID}")
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected 200 for ID={BOUNDARY_ID}, got {response.status}"

    locator = admin_page.locator("[data-testid='leave-request-id']")
    expect(locator).to_be_visible()
    expect(locator).to_have_text(str(BOUNDARY_ID))

    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_id=BOUNDARY_ID, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is not None, f"Audit record must exist for boundary entity_id={BOUNDARY_ID}"
    assert audit["entity_id"] == BOUNDARY_ID

tests.name: test_viewing_leave_request_with_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
actions: Obtain a valid admin session token via <fixture:admin_auth_token>
Construct a GET request to /leave-requests with a missing mandatory query parameter (e.g. omit required 'requestor_id')
Send the request via <helper_fn:api_client>
Assert HTTP response status is 400
Assert response body contains a validation error message referencing the missing field
expected: Response body identifies the missing mandatory field
trace: R-WEB-016 error pathway: 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_viewing_leave_request_with_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(admin_auth_token, api_client, base_url, get_audit_record_count):
    before_count = get_audit_record_count(entity_id=None, action="VIEW")

    # Omit mandatory 'requestor_id' query param to trigger validation failure
    response = api_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/leave-requests",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"},
        params={}  # intentionally empty – missing mandatory filter
    )

    assert response.status_code == 400, (
        f"Expected 400 for missing mandatory field, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    error_fields = [e.get("field", "") for e in body.get("errors", [{}])]
    assert any("requestor_id" in f for f in error_fields), (
        f"Expected validation error for 'requestor_id', got errors: {body}"
    )

    after_count = get_audit_record_count(entity_id=None, action="VIEW")
    assert after_count == before_count, "No audit record should be written on validation error"
